sentence_reviews.text: State, stability, observability, feedback, and optimal control govern systems that evolve and act over time.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Underactuated Robotics intro.html lines 62-71 and 96-111; lyapunov.html lines 30-43 and 49-51; lqr.html lines 43-58 and 69-76; AIMA contents.html Chapter 14 'States and observations' / 'Transition and sensor models' lines 361-365 and Chapter 26.5 'Planning and Control' / 'Optimal control' lines 704-716.
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected sources directly support state-based dynamical systems, stability analysis, feedback, and optimal control. Observability is only indirectly supported by AIMA's states/observations and transition/sensor-model topics, so the combined sentence is a defensible synthesis rather than exact source wording.

sentence_reviews.text: Its abstractions require explicit assumptions; a mathematically valid result can still be irrelevant to a mismatched empirical setting. Its direct dependencies include Differential equations, Linear algebra.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: Dynamical systems & control theory rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Differential equations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Underactuated Robotics intro.html lines 67-71 and 98-110; lyapunov.html lines 35-51; lqr.html lines 43-58 and 69-76.
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources repeatedly formulate control and stability problems as differential equations. The dependency is well supported, though 'rests on' is still an atlas formulation.

sentence_reviews.text: Dynamical systems & control theory rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Linear algebra.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Underactuated Robotics intro.html lines 67-71 and 91-100; lqr.html lines 21-25, 33-47, and 57-58; AIMA contents.html Appendix A.2 'Vectors, Matrices, and Linear Algebra' lines 768-774.
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources use state vectors, matrices, rank, inverses, quadratic forms, and LQR matrix equations in control. This supports the dependency, although the exact rationale is synthesized.

edge_reviews.notes: The edge direction is correct under the atlas convention: control/dynamical systems use differential equations as a prerequisite modeling language. Core strength is defensible because the inspected control notes formulate central state, stability, feedback, and optimal-control material with differential equations.
The inspected evidence supports state vectors, matrices, rank/full-row-rank conditions, inverses, quadratic forms, and LQR matrix equations as central control machinery. Core strength is defensible.
